summary: Crisp soundboard capture of the legendary 10/9/82 Stanford show – a high‑energy set highlighted by Truckin’ → The Other One, Dew and fresh 1982 material.
review: Fans rave about this patched SBD as one of the best recordings from the early‑80s, praising its crystal‑clear mix despite a few occasional overload quirks. The performance is described as “the Dead at their finest” with a blistering first set and an unstoppable second‑set jam marathon featuring Truckin’ → The Other One, Dew, Estimated Prophet, Touch of Grey and the debut of Throwing Stones/West LA Fadeaway.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews (most 5‑star) uniformly praise both musical performance and audio fidelity; only minor critiques exist, giving an overall near‑perfect show plus excellent soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with very little noise. A few reviewers note occasional bass‑drum overload or sloppy patch jumps, but overall the tape sounds clean and fully captures the band’s dynamics.

poor_songs: Deal (average version noted by a few reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight, high‑energy first set that introduces new 1982 material (Throwing Stones, Touch of Grey, West LA Fadeaway) and classic staples. The second set erupts into extended jams – Truckin’→The Other One, Dew, Drums > Space, and a soaring finish with U.S. Blues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his “banjo‑like” guitar tone, smarmy Dylan vocal moments, powerful solos and overall effortless playing; occasional lyric flubs on Touch noted but not detrimental.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for his avant‑garde bass ‘bombs’, especially during Drums > Space where his lines are described as “unbelievable”.
band_member_comments.Bob: His vocals get quirky attention (e.g., On the Road Again, lyric changes on Deal) and he adds humor with off‑the‑cuff remarks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is generally praised; a few comments mention sloppy patch jumps but also note his vocal push on Deal and solid support throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey & Bill receive glowing remarks for “strong” drumming, powerful bass‑drum hits that sometimes overload the tape, and an “unbelievable” Drums > Space segment.
